rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers that are explicitly provided in the current scope
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Ensure that helper declaration types align with the expression types
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not delete generated clauses based on diagnostic messages without verifying the requirement and AADL data representation
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
